pharusa vācā harsh.gram: glossary artifact; phrase: adjective + feminine noun
pharusa vācā harsh.note: uncertain: the headword is a run-on glossary line for pharusa vācā “harsh speech”.

pharusā.gram: adjective pharusa, feminine nominative singular (agreeing with vācā)

pharusāya.gram: adjective pharusa, feminine dative/genitive/ablative singular (with vācāya)

phoṭṭhabbā.gram: neuter noun phoṭṭhabba (future passive participle of phusati), nominative plural
breakdown: Future passive participle suffix: “what is to be touched”, the tangible.

phussattheragāthā.meaning: the Verses of the Elder Phussa (Phussattheragāthā)
breakdown: Sandhi doubling at the compound boundary.

phussattheragāthāvaṇṇanā..meaning: the commentary on the Verses of the Elder Phussa
breakdown: The Verses of the Elder Phussa.

phussitagge.meaning: blossomed at the top; with flowering crowns (of trees)
phussitagge.gram: adjective phussitagga, locative singular (or nominative plural)
breakdown: Touched, attained; (of trees) burst into blossom.
Root meaning “to touch”; phāsu describes what touches pleasantly — comfort, ease.

phāsumhā.meaning: comfortable, at ease + alas (run-together phrase phāsuṁ + hā)
phāsumhā.note: uncertain: parse follows the lexicon's division phāsuṁ + hā; the form could also be an ablative of phāsu.

breakdown: Root meaning “to place”: “to place over”, cover, close.

pilotikaṁ paribbājaka.meaning: Pilotika the wanderer (a paribbājaka, one who wanders about as a mendicant)
breakdown: Pilotika, name of a wanderer (accusative as cited).
Wandering ascetic, from paribbajati “to wander about”.
Root meaning “to drink”; the reduplicated desiderative stem pipāsa means “wanting to drink”, thirst.

pisuṇa.meaning: backbiting; malicious, slanderous (especially of speech, pisuṇa vācā)
breakdown: Root associated with crushing, grinding: speech that breaks friendships.

pisuṇa vācā.goenka: Pisuṇā vācā, backbiting or slanderous speech, is one of the four kinds of wrong speech abandoned in sammāvācā (right speech), together with lying, harsh words and idle chatter. Giving up divisive talk is part of the foundation of sīla on which the meditator's samādhi and paññā are developed.

pisuṇā.gram: adjective pisuṇa, feminine nominative singular (agreeing with vācā)

pisuṇāvācādināmameva.meaning: just the designation “malicious speech and the like”

pisuṇāya.gram: adjective pisuṇa, feminine dative/genitive singular (with vācāya)

pitā.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (mātā “mother”); only “father” belongs to pitā.

piyacakkhūhi.meaning: with affectionate eyes; with kindly eyes

piyakamyatāya.meaning: out of desire to be liked; through wanting to be dear
piyakamyatāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, compound, instrumental/dative singular

piyarūpaṁ.meaning: of pleasant form; a dear and agreeable object
piyarūpaṁ.goenka: In the Mahāsatipaṭṭhāna Sutta, taṇhā is said to arise and take root wherever in the world there is piyarūpaṁ sātarūpaṁ — whatever is pleasant and agreeable. The meditator learns by direct observation that craving springs up towards pleasant objects and sensations, and that it ceases to take root when these are observed with equanimity and the understanding of anicca.

piyataramattanā.gram: sandhi phrase: comparative adjective (accusative) + instrumental/ablative of attā
breakdown: The niggahīta joins the following vowel as m.

piyatarasuttaṁ.meaning: the Piyatara Sutta (“Discourse on What Is Dearer”)

piyataro.gram: comparative adjective of piya, nominative masculine singular

piyehi.meaning: from those who are dear; from the agreeable, liked

breakdown: What falls (into the bowl): piṇḍapāta is the alms food / alms round.
Returned, come back (past participle of paṭikkamati).

piṇḍāya for.gram: glossary artifact; masculine noun piṇḍa, dative singular
piṇḍāya for.note: uncertain: the headword is a run-on glossary line for piṇḍāya “for alms”.

piṭakasampadānena.meaning: on the authority of the scriptures; according to the tradition of the texts (piṭaka)
breakdown: Basket; collection of scriptures (as in Tipiṭaka).
piṭakasampadānena.goenka: This phrase occurs in the Kālāma Sutta's list of insufficient grounds for accepting a teaching: not by oral tradition, not by lineage, not piṭakasampadānena — on the authority of texts. The tradition emphasises this teaching strongly: truth must be realised by direct experience within oneself, not accepted out of scriptural authority or blind faith.

pokkharaṇirāmaṇeyyakaṁ.meaning: the loveliness of lotus ponds; delightful lotus ponds

ponobbhavikā.meaning: leading to renewed existence; productive of rebirth
ponobbhavikā.gram: adjective, compound (from punabbhava), feminine nominative singular
breakdown: Relating to bhava, existence; sandhi doubling of b: leading again to becoming.
ponobbhavikā.goenka: Ponobbhavikā describes taṇhā in the Second Noble Truth: yāyaṁ taṇhā ponobbhavikā — “that craving which leads to renewed existence”. Each reaction of craving generates new saṅkhāra and keeps the wheel of becoming turning; by observing sensations with equanimity the meditator stops generating this rebirth-producing craving.

breakdown: Town, city: porī is town-bred, hence refined, courteous talk.

posehi.gram: verb, 2nd person singular imperative of poseti
breakdown: Stem of poseti “to feed, to nourish”.
posehi.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (pharusa vācā “harsh speech”); only “feed, nourish” belongs to posehi.

pothujjaniko.meaning: belonging to ordinary people; worldly, of the common worldling

pubbapetakathaṁ.meaning: talk about departed relatives; tales of the former dead
breakdown: Talk (accusative); one item in the stock list of frivolous talk (tiracchānakathā) a meditator avoids.

pubbaṇhasamayaṁ.meaning: in the forenoon; during the morning time
pubbaṇhasamayaṁ.note: uncertain: the lexicon's gloss line runs into the next entry (puratthimo “eastern”); only “forenoon time” belongs to this headword.

pubbupakāritāya.meaning: because of being a former benefactor; on account of earlier help given
pubbupakāritāya.gram: feminine abstract noun, compound, instrumental/dative singular
breakdown: Abstract noun suffix -tā with oblique ending: “the state of being”.

pubbuṭṭhāyino.gram: adjective pubbuṭṭhāyī, nominative plural (or genitive singular)
breakdown: Before, earlier (pubbe + u coalesce to pubbu-).
One who rises, gets up (from uṭṭhahati).

pucchāmi.gram: verb, 1st person singular present of pucchati (√pucch)

puggalavaggo.meaning: the Puggala Vagga (“Chapter on Persons”), a section presenting details about the individuals discussed
puggalavaggo.gram: proper noun (chapter title), masculine nominative singular
